raw_consumer_statement:
I rented a car from XXXX for one day on XX/XX/year>. The payment was prepaid, and I used my AMEX card only as the security deposit. When I returned the car the next day on time, the drop-off location had very limited parking. I had to wait about 40 minutes outside the lot before a staff member could process the return. Despite this delay being entirely due to XXXX  lack of space and staffing, I was charged an additional {$77.00} as an extra hour fee. 

I immediately called XXXX customer service, and the agent confirmed that a refund would be issued. The following day, I also emailed XXXX, and their team replied in writing, promising that the refund would be processed within 5 business days. However, I never received the refund. 

As a result, I filed a dispute with American Express. Unfortunately, AMEX closed the case after XXXX provided only the original receipt showing the {$77.00} charge, without reviewing or considering the email evidence from XXXX where they admitted and confirmed the refund. This outcome is unacceptable, as critical evidence was ignored.
